perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge that, in the course of thei r consideration of and any concurrent or subsequent discussions between CVS and Vendor or th eir representatives relating to the Project, each party may receive certain non-public and confi dential information from or about the other party or its affiliates, including but not limited to tec hnical, financial, and business information and mod els, names of potential customers or partners, proposed business deals, reports, plans, market projections, software programs, data, or any other confidential and proprietary information relating t o the Project. All such technical, financial, or oth er business information thus supplied by either par ty to the other or the other's representatives is here inafter called the "Information". The term "Information" as used herein also includes (i) the fact that the Information has been made available t o or is being inspected or evaluated by the receiving party, (ii) the fact that such discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Projec t or other related transactions between CVS and Vendor, and (iii) any of the terms, conditions, or other facts with respect to the Project or other re lated transactions, including the status thereof. Any In formation supplied by either party to the other pri or to the execution of this Agreement shall be conside red in the same manner and be subject to the same treatment as the Information made available af ter the execution of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge that, in the course of thei r consideration of and any concurrent or subsequent discussions between CVS and Vendor or th eir representatives relating to the Project, each party may receive information from or about the other party or its affiliates. All information thus supplied by either par ty to the other or the other's representatives is hereinafter called the "Information". Any In formation supplied by either party to the other pri or to the execution of this Agreement shall be conside red in the same manner and be subject to the same treatment as the Information made available af ter the execution of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific definition of 'non-public and confidential information' and subsequent examples, the term 'Information' becomes vague. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 4 refers to CVS Information with specific obligations regarding its protection, but without a clear definition in Section 2, it's unclear what constitutes 'CVS Information', creating an enforcement problem.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party receiving any Information shall keep suc h Information confidential and shall not disclose such Information, in whole or in part, to any perso n other than its representatives who need to know such Information in connection with the receiving p arty's evaluation thereof and determination of business strategies, or other actions in connection with the Project (it being agreed and understood that such representatives shall be informed by the receiving party of the confidential nature of the Information and shall be required by the receiving party to agree to treat the Information 2 - Revised May 7, 2010 confidentially), except with the prior written cons ent of the other party hereto or as otherwise permitted hereunder. Vendor is strictly prohibited from placing any CVS Information on portable computing/storage devices, which are not owned and secured by Vendor. Vendor will take all reasonable, necessary, and appropriate measures, including encryption, to ensure that CVS Information stored on Vendor owned and secured devices cannot be accessed by una uthorized/inappropriate individuals. Vendor further agrees to monitor networks, systems, and ph ysical plant for violations of this Agreement and to take the appropriate technical and procedural ac tions to ensure that any CVS Information is completely removed from any device, technologies, a nd/or locations in the event of any violation of security policy or procedure or any perceived infra ction thereof. Vendor shall give CVS notice immediately if CVS Information has been accessed by unauthorized individuals or if there is any evidence of any such infraction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific definition of 'non-public and confidential information' from Section 2, making the term 'Information' vague. Section 4, the contradicted text, refers to 'CVS Information' and imposes specific obligations regarding its protection. Without a clear definition in Section 2, it's unclear what constitutes 'CVS Information', creating an enforcement problem and a direct contradiction, as the scope of what must be kept confidential is now undefined. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor will take all reasonable, necessary, and appropriate measures, including encryption, to ensure that CVS Information stored on Vendor owned and secured devices cannot be accessed by una uthorized/inappropriate individuals. Vendor further agrees to monitor networks, systems, and ph ysical plant for violations of this Agreement and to take the appropriate technical and procedural ac tions to ensure that any CVS Information is completely removed from any device, technologies, a nd/or locations in the event of any violation of security policy or procedure or any perceived infra ction thereof. Vendor shall give CVS notice immediately if CVS Information has been accessed by unauthorized individuals or if there is any evidence of any such infraction.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor will take reasonable measures to ensure that CVS Information stored on Vendor owned devices cannot be accessed by unauthorized individuals. Vendor shall give CVS notice immediately if CVS Information has been accessed by unauthorized individuals or if there is any evidence of any such infraction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the terms 'necessary', 'appropriate', and the specific measure of 'encryption,' along with removing 'secured', the obligation on Vendor is weakened. This creates a contradiction because Section 6 requires a 'Standard of Protection' using efforts 'commensurate with those that such party employs for the protection of corresponding sensitive information of its own'. The modified Section 4 now lacks specific security measures that align with that standard.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purpose of complying with the obligations s et forth herein, the party receiving any Informatio n shall use efforts commensurate with those that such party employs for the protection of corresponding sensitive information of its own, and such receiving party shall not be liable for any inadvertent disclosure of Information provided that (i) it has used substantially the same degree of care to avoid disclosing such Information as it use s for its own information of like importance, and ( ii) upon discovery of any inadvertent disclosure it sha ll use reasonable efforts to prevent further disclosure of such Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the security obligations of the Vendor by removing the terms 'necessary', 'appropriate', 'encryption', and 'secured'. The contradicted text requires the Vendor to use efforts 'commensurate with those that such party employs for the protection of corresponding sensitive information of its own'. The omission of specific security measures in the changed text directly contradicts this requirement, as it suggests a lower standard of protection than what the contradicted section mandates, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor is strictly prohibited from placing any CVS Information on portable computing/storage devices, which are not owned and secured by Vendor.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor is discouraged from placing any CVS Information on portable computing/storage devices, which are not owned by Vendor.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'strictly prohibited' to 'discouraged' weakens the restriction. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 9, which states that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this agreement, and Section 4 stating that if there's violation action will be taken, but how can you seek damage when the word being used is 'discouraged' which create a doubt that it is a violation?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and agrees that money damage s would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that the other party s hall be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or forestall any such br each or threatened breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or i n equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text 'strictly prohibited' created a firm obligation on the vendor regarding CVS Information and portable devices. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'discouraged'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that monetary damages are insufficient for any breach and injunctive relief should be sought. Since the changed text weakens the initial restriction to 'discouraged' it creates an in-text contradiction because if the vendor is only 'discouraged' and not 'strictly prohibited,' it becomes unclear whether a violation even occurred, thus directly contradicting the clause that monetary damages are insufficient for any breach of the agreement, and action should be taken. This is an Omissions - In Text Contradiction type.

perturbation.original_text: All Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof), shall remain the property of the furnishing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Tangible copies of the Information shall remain the property of the furnishing party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'computerized or electronic versions thereof', the agreement becomes ambiguous regarding the ownership of digital copies. This contradicts Section 8, where the definition does not include those file types, but those file types are critical for CVS, since they have to provide the information, that later on, if the other party infringes the clause, there's an in-text contradiction because the infringed information isn't being protected under the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No license to a party, under any trademark, patent, copyright, mask work protection right, or any other intellectual property right, is either grante d or implied by the conveying of Information to suc h party. All Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof), shall remain the property of the furnishing party. Within ten (10) days following the receipt of a written request referencing this Agreement and this paragraph from either party furnishing Information hereunder, the receiving party will del iver to the furnishing party all tangible materials containing or embodying the Information received fr om the furnishing party, except for materials containing Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilations, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receivin g party or its representatives, together with a certificate executed by the receiving party certify ing that all such materials in the receiving party' s 3 - Revised May 7, 2010 possession have been delivered to the furnishing pa rty or destroyed. That portion of the Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilat ions, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its representati ves shall be held by the receiving party and kept confidential as provided above, or shall be destroy ed.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'All Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof), shall remain the property of the furnishing party'. The changed text removes 'computerized or electronic versions thereof'. The contradicted text also states 'All Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof), shall remain the property of the furnishing party'. The changed text creates a contradiction because it no longer includes computerized or electronic versions in the definition of Information that remains the property of the furnishing party, while the contradicted text does include them. This omission directly conflicts with the contradicted text, creating an in-text contradiction. According to the Perturbation explanation, this omission creates an in-text contradiction because the file types are not being protected under the clause.

perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) days following the receipt of a written request referencing this Agreement and this paragraph from either party furnishing Information hereunder, the receiving party will del iver to the furnishing party all tangible materials containing or embodying the Information received fr om the furnishing party, except for materials containing Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilations, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receivin g party or its representatives, together with a certificate executed by the receiving party certify ing that all such materials in the receiving party' s 3 - Revised May 7, 2010 possession have been delivered to the furnishing pa rty or destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Following a written request referencing this Agreement from either party furnishing Information hereunder, the receiving party will return the Information, except for materials containing Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilations, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receivin g party or its representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specific timeframe of 'Within ten (10) days' creates ambiguity. This now contradicts Section 11, which specifies a term and termination of the agreement and how long the party has to maintain confidentiality, creating doubt whether the terms are applicable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may terminate the exchange of Informat ion under this Agreement at any time by written notice to the other specifically referencing this A greement. In any event, however, the obligations o f each party to maintain the confidentiality of the I nformation it has received under this Agreement shall continue for the later to occur of (i) a peri od of three (3) years after such termination, or (i i) if this Agreement is incorporated by reference into another agreement between the parties, the term, including extensions, of that agreement, plus three (3) years.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 10-day timeframe for returning tangible materials. The changed text removes this timeframe, creating ambiguity. The contradicted text in Section 11 establishes a period of three years for maintaining confidentiality after termination. The removal of the 10-day timeframe introduces a contradiction because it's now unclear whether Section 11's longer timeframe applies to the return of tangible materials, thus creating a conflict and inconsistency in how the obligations are meant to be fulfilled after termination, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation, fitting the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or i n equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Such remedy shall be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy' to 'shall be the exclusive remedy' directly contradicts the implied rights and remedies that may be available at law or in equity. This contradiction impacts the enforceability of the contract because, previously, parties could pursue legal avenues beyond injunctive relief. Now, if a breach causes significant damages not addressed by an injunction, the injured party is limited, which contradicts the initial intent to provide comprehensive protection, in violation to section 14 that states, Governing Law This Agreement shall be governed by and construed a nd enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed a nd enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its choice of law provisions.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the remedy is 'not exclusive,' preserving other legal options. The changed text makes the remedy 'exclusive,' directly contradicting the enforceability and rights granted by New York law as stated in the contradicted section, which requires the agreement to be 'enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.' The change limits the avenues of legal recourse that would otherwise be available under New York law, which is a direct contradiction.
